stories.headline: Schwab opens spot BTC and ETH trading to retail brokerage clients
stories.tldr: Charles Schwab flipped the switch on direct spot Bitcoin and Ethereum trading inside its retail brokerage, the deepest TradFi push into crypto since the ETFs.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Schwab brings $9T+ in client assets and a non-crypto-native user base into spot rails, sidestepping the ETF wrapper and pressuring Coinbase's retail moat.
the_details: Schwab launched direct spot BTC and ETH trading across its retail brokerage platform in April 2026, putting a fourth megabank-scale broker in the spot market.
Move follows a year of IBIT-led ETF inflows and arrives as Nomura's 2026 survey shows 80% of institutions plan 2-5% AUM allocations to digital assets.
Coinbase shares now face a direct on-platform competitor for retail spot flow, the segment that drives the bulk of CB's take-rate.

stories.headline: April crypto theft tops $620M in 20 days, worst month since Bybit's $1.4B
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols have lost over $620M to hacks in the first 20 days of April, making it the worst month for theft since Bybit's $1.4B breach in February 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Bridge failures and admin-key exploits — not novel zero-days — drove the carnage, signaling that DeFi's known weak points still aren't being hardened.
the_details: Two incidents account for the bulk: Drift Protocol drained for $285M on April 1 and Kelp DAO drained for $293M on April 19 via a LayerZero bridge exploit.
Authorities have linked both attacks to North Korea's Lazarus Group, with Drift attributed to a long-running social engineering campaign using pre-signed authorizations.
Smaller April hits include CoW Swap ($1.2M domain hijack on April 14) and Russia-linked Grinex ($13.74M USDT drain on April 15).

stories.headline: SEC frees DEX interface providers from broker-dealer rules in April 13 staff letter
stories.tldr: SEC staff said it won't object to 'Covered User Interface Providers' offering software for crypto-asset-security transactions without registering as broker-dealers, subject to conditions.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: It's the clearest US legal cover yet for DEX front-ends, defusing the existential threat that built up under the prior administration's enforcement posture.
the_details: April 13, 2026 staff statement carves out interfaces that prepare and submit transactions in crypto asset securities, provided they meet a set of disclosure and non-custody conditions.
Builds on the March 17 SEC-CFTC joint interpretive release that imposed a five-category token taxonomy and superseded the 2019 framework.
Coinbase, Ripple and 100+ firms are simultaneously pressing the Senate Banking Committee to mark up the Clarity Act to lock the framework into statute.

stories.headline: BlackRock's IBIT options open interest tops Deribit in institutional milestone
stories.tldr: Open interest in BlackRock's spot Bitcoin ETF options surpassed Deribit's BTC options book on Friday, marking the moment regulated US derivatives leapfrogged the offshore venue.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Liquidity migrating onshore to a CFTC-touched venue accelerates the institutional bid for vol-selling strategies and undercuts Deribit's pricing power.
the_details: IBIT options OI overtook Deribit's BTC OI on Friday, just 16 months after launch — Deribit had dominated crypto options since 2019.
Bitcoin whales on Hyperliquid have built a steadily long bias since February despite deeply negative funding, with BTC tagging $80,000.
Kevin O'Leary, citing scale and survivability, disclosed his crypto book is now ~90% BTC and ETH after dumping altcoin exposure.

stories.headline: Bitcoin eyes best month in a year, up 13% as $5B USDT mint fuels rebound
stories.tldr: Bitcoin is on track for its strongest April in a year, holding above $77K and up 13% on the month after $5B in fresh USDT supply hit the market.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Stablecoin issuance is the cleanest leading indicator of crypto-native buying power, and a $5B USDT print this size historically front-runs sustained spot bids.
the_details: BTC's 30-day correlation with the DXY hit -0.90, the most negative reading since 2022, framing the rally as a dollar-weakness trade.
Tether minted roughly $5B in fresh USDT during April, the largest monthly increase of 2026 so far.
April 26 saw 3.73% of Plasma's (XPL) supply unlock — about $10.1M — adding short-term sell pressure to one altcoin amid an otherwise BTC-led tape.
